name: Charlie Kirk
summary: Charlie Kirk's character and pattern of behavior are marked by a consistent promotion of misinformation, conspiracy theories, and extremist ideologies. He has repeatedly spread false claims about various topics, including COVID-19, voter fraud, and social issues, and has used his platform to amplify hate speech and discriminatory rhetoric. Kirk has also demonstrated a willingness to engage in dishonest and manipulative tactics, including deleting tweets and denying previous statements. His actions and words have been criticized for threatening academic freedom, targeting marginalized communities, and undermining democratic institutions. Overall, Kirk's behavior suggests a disregard for truth, facts, and human dignity, and a commitment to advancing a radical and divisive agenda.

entries.fact: Charlie Kirk co-founded the conservative student organization Turning Point USA, which later expanded into affiliate groups such as Turning Point Action and Turning Point Faith, promoting Republican causes and conservative positions, including opposition to abortion, gun control, DEI programs, and LGBTQ rights.

Charlie Kirk said in an interview with Wired during the 2016 Republican National Convention that he would vote for Donald Trump despite not being a big fan, and that Trump's candidacy made Turning Point's mission more difficult.
Charlie Kirk promoted debunked claims about George Floyd, such as that he was 'illegally counterfeiting currency' and had once 'put a gun to a pregnant woman's stomach'.
Charlie Kirk tweeted a false claim about human trafficking arrests, which originated from the conspiracy site 8chan, and later deleted it without explanation.
Charlie Kirk falsely claimed that protesters in the French yellow vests movement chanted 'We want Trump'.
Charlie Kirk's salary from Turning Point USA increased from $27,000 to nearly $300,000, and he bought an $855,000 condo in Longboat Key, Florida, while advocating for Trump.
Charlie Kirk's organization Turning Point USA published the Professor Watchlist, which critics say threatens academic freedom and has led to the targeted harassment of academics.
Charlie Kirk became chairman of Students for Trump, which had been acquired by Turning Point Action.

Charlie Kirk claimed he never said that a 'far less-qualified candidate of a different gender and a different persuasion' was accepted to the U.S. Military Academy instead of him, despite having made the statement in 2015.
Charlie Kirk promoted COVID-19 misinformation and false allegations of electoral fraud in the 2020 election.
Charlie Kirk released his book The MAGA Doctrine, a manifesto for the Make America Great Again movement, in which he described the Republican Party as a 'Trump-remade populist party'.

Charlie Kirk was described by The New York Times as exemplifying 'walking the line between mainstream conservative opinion and outright disinformation' and amplifying the president's message while helping shape it with the support of Donald Trump Jr.

Charlie Kirk said at a Council for National Policy meeting that Democrats shutting down college campuses would remove opportunities for ballot harvesting and voter fraud, and encouraged keeping campuses closed as it would be beneficial for his side.
The Falkirk Center for Faith and Liberty, co-founded by Charlie Kirk and Jerry Falwell Jr., spent at least $50,000 on Facebook advertisements promoting Trump and Republican candidates
Turning Point USA made 'misleading financial claims', and the audits were not done by an independent auditor, according to an investigation by ProPublica.

Charlie Kirk announced the launch of Turning Point Academy, an online academy intended to cater to families seeking an 'America-first education', which critics say is an attempt to promote anti-American ideas.

Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that Turning Point Action and Students for Trump were sending more than 80 'buses of patriots to D.C. to fight for this president', the day before the Washington, D.C., protest that led to the January 6 U.S. Capitol attack
Charlie Kirk's organizations received $1.25 million from Publix heiress Julie Fancelli to fund buses to the January 6 event, and he paid $60,000 for Kimberly Guilfoyle to speak at the rally
Charlie Kirk said he was 'getting 500 emails a minute calling for a civil war' in the lead-up to the storming of the Capitol
Turning Point Academy partnered with Dream City Christian School, a private school that received $900,000 in Arizona school voucher funds in the 2022-2023 school year.
Charlie Kirk called for a 'patriot' to bail out of jail the man who broke into Nancy Pelosi's house and attacked and tried to murder her husband with a hammer.
Charlie Kirk pleaded the Fifth Amendment privilege against self-incrimination when appearing before the U.S. House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack
Charlie Kirk called for the imprisonment or the death penalty of Joe Biden for 'crimes against America'.

A Brookings Institution study found Charlie Kirk's podcast contained the second-highest proportion of false, misleading, and unsubstantiated statements among 36,603 episodes produced by 79 prominent political podcasters.
Charlie Kirk advocated for Christian nationalism and criticized the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and Martin Luther King Jr.
Charlie Kirk promoted the white genocide conspiracy theory.
Charlie Kirk repeatedly promoted the false claim that the medical examiner who performed George Floyd's autopsy declared he had died of an overdose.
Charlie Kirk was appointed by Trump to the U.S. Air Force Academy Board of Visitors.
Charlie Kirk expressed concerns that the Trump administration's crackdowns on campuses threatened free speech and were a weaponization of antisemitism.
Charlie Kirk stated that it should be legal to burn a rainbow or Black Lives Matter flag in public.
Charlie Kirk asserted that the increasing presence of African Americans in American politics resulted in the Black community being 'worse off'.
Charlie Kirk said he believes marriage is one man, one woman, but added that gay people should be allowed in the conservative movement.
Charlie Kirk spread falsehoods about voter fraud ahead of the 2020 U.S. presidential election.
Charlie Kirk was considered a 'big name' social influencer in Rudy Giuliani's communications plan to overturn the 2020 election.
Charlie Kirk spread false information and conspiracy theories about COVID-19 on social media, including the claim that hydroxychloroquine was '100% effective in treating the virus'.

Charlie Kirk alleged that the WHO covered up information about the COVID-19 pandemic and that Gretchen Whitmer, the Democratic governor of Michigan, threatened doctors who tried to use hydroxychloroquine.
Charlie Kirk falsely stated that during the 2009 swine flu pandemic it 'took President Barack Obama millions infected and over 1,000 deaths' to declare a public health emergency.

Charlie Kirk described the public health measure of social distancing prohibitions in churches as a Democratic plot against Christianity and made the unfounded assertion that authorities in Wuhan, China, were burning patients.
Charlie Kirk said he refused to abide by mask requirements because 'the science around masks is very questionable'.
Charlie Kirk led a Stop the Steal protest at the Maricopa Tabulation Center in Phoenix, promoting false and disproven claims of fraud in the election.
Charlie Kirk promoted misleading claims about the efficacy and safety of COVID-19 vaccines.
Charlie Kirk called mandatory requirements for students to take the COVID-19 vaccine 'medical apartheid'.

Charlie Kirk said on his podcast that Democrats wanted Americans to live where 'there is no cultural identity, where you live in sexual anarchy, where private property is a thing of the past, and the ruling class controls everything'.
Charlie Kirk called the separation of church and state in the United States a 'fabrication'.
Charlie Kirk routinely made anti-LGBTQ remarks, opposing transgender rights and medical care, and asserted that there is an 'LGBTQ agenda'.
Charlie Kirk claimed that being gay was an 'error' and likened homosexuality to drug addiction.

Charlie Kirk criticized the Supreme Court's decision in Obergefell v. Hodges, calling LGBTQ activists the 'alphabet mafia' and claiming that the movement is not 'just about two dudes being able to get married'.

Charlie Kirk said that conservatives mistakenly thought the issue of same-sex marriage in the United States would end after the ruling, instead concluding that '...[gay people] are not happy just having marriage. Instead, they now want to corrupt your children'.
Charlie Kirk said that transgender women in women's locker rooms should be 'taken care of the way we used to take care of things in the 1950s and '60s'.

Charlie Kirk said, 'One issue I think that is so against our senses, so against the natural law and, dare I say, a throbbing middle finger to God, is the transgender thing happening in America right now.'

Charlie Kirk said that 'all men are created equal in the eyes of God, all men and women, but not all cultures are created equal. To say that, you get attacked in every direction, but excuse me when I say that Western civilization is the best that humanity has produced. It's an outgrowth of the Bible.'

Charlie Kirk said, 'I think it's worth it, I think it's worth to have a cost of, unfortunately, some gun deaths every single year so that we can have the Second Amendment to protect our other God-given rights.'

Charlie Kirk said, 'One of the reasons we're living through a constitutional crisis is that we no longer have a Christian nation, but we have a Christian form of government, and they're incompatible. You cannot have liberty if you do not have a Christian population.'
Charlie Kirk said, 'This is a Christian state. I'd like to see it stay that way.'
Charlie Kirk called for Trump to propose a nationwide ban of gender-affirming care for transgender people.
Charlie Kirk called for the imprisonment of doctors who perform gender-affirming care and demanded 'Nuremberg-style' trials for them.

Charlie Kirk criticized YouTuber Ms. Rachel for a post that celebrated Pride Month by quoting the Bible verse 'love thy neighbor', arguing that she was being selective and referencing a verse that says 'thou shall lay with another man shall be stoned to death'.
Charlie Kirk argued that abortion is murder and should be illegal, and opposed exceptions for rape, including for children as young as 10.
Charlie Kirk compared abortion to the Holocaust, and said that abortion is worse.

Charlie Kirk called for the elimination of Jasmine Crockett's congressional district, justifying the erasure of her district by claiming she was a part of an 'attempt to eliminate the white population in this country'.

Charlie Kirk co-wrote the book Time for a Turning Point: Setting a Course Toward Free Markets and Limited Government for Future Generations, which was published by Post Hill Press, a subsidiary of Simon & Schuster.

Charlie Kirk and TPUSA's communication director Candace Owens presented what the Southern Poverty Law Center described as 'a symphony of racist dog-whistles ... pandering to the extreme right' at the 2018 Western Conservative Summit.
Charlie Kirk told a college audience that the concept of white privilege is a myth and a 'racist idea'.
Charlie Kirk was a speaker for the annual conference held by the anti-Muslim advocacy group ACT for America in Washington DC
Charlie Kirk promoted traditional gender roles, telling young women to find husbands who are 'a protector and a leader, and deep down, a vast majority of you agree'.

Charlie Kirk told followers of his radio podcast that Democratic immigration policies were aimed at 'diminishing and decreasing white demographics in America' and called for Texas to 'deputize a citizen force and put them on the border' to protect 'white demographics in America'.
Charlie Kirk began the 'Exposing Critical Racism Tour' of a number of campuses and off-campus venues to 'fight racist theories on America's college campuses!'
Charlie Kirk told young women to go to college for the purpose of finding husbands and 'embrace their roles as mothers and homemakers'.
Charlie Kirk argued that 'It's not Islamophobia to notice that Muslims want to import values into the West that seek to destabilize our civilization.'
Charlie Kirk said that 'prowling Blacks go around for fun to go target white people' in urban America.

Charlie Kirk reacted to Representative Sheila Jackson Lee's statement on affirmative action by stating, 'If we would have said three weeks ago [...] that Joy Reid and Michelle Obama and Sheila Jackson Lee and Ketanji Brown Jackson were affirmative-action picks, we would have been called racist. But now they're comin' out and they're saying it for us!'
Charlie Kirk called on American Jews to stop 'subsidizing your own demise by supporting institutions that breed Anti-Semites and endorse genocidal killers'.
Charlie Kirk said that 'Jewish donors have been the number 1 funding mechanism of radical, open border, neoliberal, quasi-Marxist policies' and suggested that these Jews control various institutions.
Charlie Kirk condemned the Civil Rights Act of 1964, calling its passage a 'huge mistake' and alleging that it had created a 'permanent DEI-type bureaucracy'.
Charlie Kirk described Martin Luther King Jr. as 'awful ... not a good person' and as someone who is admired only because he said 'one thing he didn't actually believe'.
Charlie Kirk called Ketanji Brown Jackson a 'recipient of affirmative action' and said she was nominated for the Supreme Court because of her race.
Charlie Kirk claimed that Martin Luther King Jr. was 'the most honored, worshiped, even deified person of the 20th century' despite 'most people' supposedly disliking him during his life.
Charlie Kirk said that a Black person had to 'steal a white person's slot' to be taken seriously.
Charlie Kirk said that if he saw a Black pilot, he would wonder if the pilot was qualified.
Charlie Kirk said, 'The American Democrat party hates this country. They wanna see it collapse. They love it when America becomes less white.'

Charlie Kirk posted 'The ' Great Replacement ' is not a theory, it's a reality', alongside a Fox News headline that falsely claimed: '7.2M illegals entered the U.S. under Biden administration, an amount greater than population of 36 states.'
Charlie Kirk said, 'The great replacement strategy, which is well under way every single day in our southern border, is a strategy to replace white rural America with something different'.
Charlie Kirk commented that America would still be America if it were ethnically 90% Indian, as long as they were Christian Indians.
Charlie Kirk elaborated on Hinduism and his disapproval of its morality due to its polytheism, stating that when you have multiple gods, you get different moralities.

Charlie Kirk stated that 'America does not need more visas for people from India', arguing that Indian-American immigrants dominate the American workforce and decrease job opportunities for Americans.
Charlie Kirk posted a tweet following the victory of Zohran Mamdani in the 2025 New York City Democratic mayoral primary, which was described as gross and Islamophobic
Charlie Kirk blamed the high death toll of the Central Texas floods on DEI programs.
Charlie Kirk claimed that the philosophical foundation of anti-whiteness has been largely financed by Jewish donors
Charlie Kirk wrote on Twitter that Islam is the sword the left is using to slit the throat of America

Charlie Kirk accused Democrats of spreading a 'false narrative' that there is a relentless assault against Black people by white people, and claimed that white individuals are more likely to be attacked by Black individuals.
Charlie Kirk opposed the Paris Agreement on climate change.
Charlie Kirk admitted that Turning Point USA had accepted funding from the fossil fuel industry and spoke out against targeting fossil fuels.
Charlie Kirk and Candace Owens claimed in a video that there is no factual data to back up global warming, which was rated inaccurate by Science Feedback.
Charlie Kirk advanced pro-Russian talking points about the Russo-Ukrainian War.
Charlie Kirk warned that climate activism would erode American sovereignty and private property, describing it as a Trojan Horse for Marxism and likening it to pseudo-paganism.
Charlie Kirk said that the southern border matters more than the Ukrainian border and called for Republican leaders to label the situation at the southern border an invasion.
Charlie Kirk said of Palestine, 'I don't think the place exists.'
Charlie Kirk blamed the Chinese government and drug cartels for the opioid crisis in the United States.
Charlie Kirk called for the use of force against migrants at the U.S.–Mexico border, including the use of tear gas, rubber bullets, and whips.
Charlie Kirk denied that Israel is starving Palestinians.
Charlie Kirk said that immigration to the United States should be completely stopped.
Charlie Kirk called Mehdi Hasan a 'neurotic lunatic' and said, 'Send him back to the country he came from. Holy cow! Get him off TV. Revoke his visa.'
Charlie Kirk opposed U.S. involvement in the Iran–Israel War.
Charlie Kirk opposed the U.S. sending arms to Ukraine or providing financial support to the country.
Charlie Kirk called U.S. representative Ilhan Omar a 'terrorist sympathizer' and called for her deportation.

Charlie Kirk promoted a conspiracy theory alleging the Israeli government knew that Hamas was going to launch the attack, and that Benjamin Netanyahu allowed it to go ahead as part of a plan to remain in power.
Charlie Kirk promoted the false claim that Haitians in Springfield, Ohio, were eating residents' pets and other wildlife.
Charlie Kirk promoted the false claim that Haitians in Springfield, Ohio, were eating residents' pets and other wildlife.

Charlie Kirk offered an 'apology' to the Russian people, stating 'very few Americans want war with you' and that 'the people obsessed with fighting you forever' were a minority 'on their way out of power'.
Charlie Kirk called Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy a 'CIA puppet' and 'gangster' who 'sent his own people to a senseless massacre'.
Charlie Kirk stated that if the US took Taiwan, it would probably start a nuclear war and that the US should have taken Taiwan in 1950.
Charlie Kirk promoted climate change denial, calling global warming a hoax.
Charlie Kirk opposed a bipartisan bill to expand anti-BDS laws, which punish the boycott of Israel.
Charlie Kirk said the bill to expand anti-BDS laws would 'only create more antisemitism, and play into growing narratives that Israel is running the U.S. government'.
Charlie Kirk disagreed with Trump's decision to send more military aid to Ukraine.

Far-right activists, including Laura Loomer, called for violence and retaliation in the aftermath of Charlie Kirk's assassination and doxxed people they accused of celebrating or justifying Kirk's death.
Trump administration officials and congressional Republicans immediately blamed Democrats and liberal beliefs for Charlie Kirk's shooting without evidence
Trump and congressional Republicans received criticism for exploiting Charlie Kirk's death for political gain

The Trump administration demanded severe penalties for individuals responsible for Charlie Kirk's assassination and instructed staff to identify and discipline service members who mocked or expressed approval of Kirk's murder

Over 100 people, including lawyers, doctors, first responders, and more than 50 high school teachers and college professors, had been censured, suspended, dismissed, or were under investigation for criticizing Charlie Kirk or expressing approval of his murder

Charlie Kirk wrote the book Campus Battlefield: How Conservatives Can WIN the Battle on Campus and Why It Matters, which was published by Post Hill Press, a subsidiary of Simon & Schuster, and described by Adam Rubenstein as a 'hot mess' and 'nothing more than a marketing pitch for TPUSA'.

Charlie Kirk wrote the book The MAGA Doctrine: The Only Ideas That Will Win the Future, which was published by HarperCollins and criticized by Gabriel Debenedetti for its inconsistent and unrealistic ideas, and by Steve Donoghue for containing 'something that's either trivially, casually wrong ... or just bipartisanly ridiculous' on every page.
Charlie Kirk used the COVID-19 virus as a cudgel to further his conservative agenda
Charlie Kirk's organization, Turning Point USA, was found to have questionable financial reporting and insiders were cashing in
Charlie Kirk was recorded in a closed-door session telling conservatives to not be afraid of being accused of being voter suppressors
Charlie Kirk's Twitter account was locked for spreading misinformation about mail-in votes
Charlie Kirk deleted a tweet saying he sent '80+ buses full of patriots' to D.C. before the Capitol siege
Charlie Kirk said the Capitol attack was not an insurrection
Charlie Kirk and Candace Owens falsely claimed there is no evidence of global warming and scientists don't know the cause in a viral Turning Point USA video
Charlie Kirk's pro-Trump youth group, Turning Point USA, was stoking vaccine resistance as COVID-19 cases surged
Charlie Kirk likened concern about climate change to 'pseudo-paganism'
Charlie Kirk stated that Joe Biden should be 'put in prison and/or given the death penalty for crimes against America'

Charlie Kirk wrote the book Right Wing Revolution: How to Beat the Woke and Save the West, in which he argues that the United States is 'under threat from a lethal ideology that seeks to humiliate and erase anyone that does not bow at its altar'.
Charlie Kirk promoted Christian Nationalism and far-right politically conservative politics through his organization TPUSA
Charlie Kirk used his platforms to decry racial equity programs, float conspiracy theories, and test divisive messaging
Charlie Kirk was described as a Christian nationalist by Kyle Spencer in his book 'Raising Them Right'
Charlie Kirk pushed conspiracies about election fraud, Christian nationalism, anti-immigrant xenophobia, and racism
Charlie Kirk spoke at the RNC as a far-right conservative talk show host
Charlie Kirk's online show was found to be spreading climate misinformation
Charlie Kirk's rhetoric was cast as racist, xenophobic, and extreme by groups that study hate speech, including the Southern Poverty Law Center
Charlie Kirk made comments about 'prowling blacks' and had views on gun control
Charlie Kirk floated a conspiracy theory that Netanyahu issued a 'stand down' order as a means to bolster his own political standing after the October 7th attacks
